Container.Name: sre-agent
Container.Overview:
Auto-diagnoses homelab incidents. Receives Alertmanager webhooks, gathers logs (Loki), metrics (Prometheus), and container state around the incident, asks Claude what most likely went wrong, and pushes a plain-language diagnosis to your phone via ntfy. Read-only by design: Docker is reached only through a GET-only socket proxy and there is no auto-remediation.
REQUIRES an existing monitoring stack: Prometheus, Loki (with promtail or similar shipping container logs), and Alertmanager routing a webhook receiver at http://[this container]:8080/webhook. All are available in Community Applications. Also requires a GET-only Docker socket proxy (e.g. the "docker-socket-proxy" CA app with only CONTAINERS=1 granted) and an Anthropic API key. Without the monitoring stack the container starts and does nothing.
Optional: an MCP server on a second port lets Claude clients chat about homelab status. It has NO authentication, by design: only ever bind it to a Tailscale/VPN interface, never the LAN or the internet.

Config.Name: ntfy topic
Config.Description: ntfy topic to push diagnoses to. Treat the topic name as a secret. Empty disables notifications.

Config.Name: ntfy server
Config.Description: ntfy server base URL (self-hosted or ntfy.sh).

Config.Name: Loki URL
Config.Description: Loki base URL. Container logs must be shipped to Loki (promtail) for diagnoses to see them.

Config.Name: Loki container label
Config.Description: Loki stream label that holds Docker container names (promtail's docker_sd usually exposes container_name).

Config.Name: Docker socket proxy URL
Config.Description: GET-only Docker socket proxy (e.g. tecnativa/docker-socket-proxy with CONTAINERS=1, POST=0). Never point this at the raw Docker socket.

Config.Name: Triage model
Config.Description: Cheap model used for the first-pass diagnosis of every incident.

Config.Name: Escalation model
Config.Description: Stronger model used when triage confidence is below the threshold.

Config.Name: Confidence threshold
Config.Description: Escalate to the stronger model below this triage confidence (0-1).

Config.Name: Log byte budget
Config.Description: Max bytes of logs included in each diagnosis context (newest kept).

Config.Name: Incident memory window (days)
Config.Description: How far back prior incidents are recalled into new diagnoses.

Config.Name: Incident memory max entries
Config.Description: Max prior incidents recalled per diagnosis (0 disables incident memory).

Config.Name: Tool budget
Config.Description: Max read-only tool calls the escalation model may make per incident (0 disables tools).

Config.Name: MCP listen address
Config.Description: Optional MCP server listen address, e.g. :8082 (empty = disabled). WARNING: no authentication by design. Publish the port ONLY on a Tailscale/VPN interface, never on the LAN or the internet. See ADR-0002 in the project docs.

Config.Name: Database path
Config.Description: SQLite incident store path inside the container. Keep it under /data so history survives updates.

Config.Name: Webhook port
Config.Description: Alertmanager webhook receiver. Point an Alertmanager webhook_configs url at http://[IP]:[PORT:8080]/webhook

Config.Name: Data
Config.Description: Incident history (SQLite). Must be writable by UID 1000.
